title: Moussaka
excerpt: This recipe looks difficult, but it's ridiculously easy. And the best part is: it should actually be made the day before so everything can come together nicely. The next day you just warm it slowly in the oven and serve. This one's a winner! Ingredients Vegetables 4 large potatoes …
html:
This recipe looks difficult, but it's ridiculously easy. And the best part is: it should actually be made the day before so everything can come together nicely. The next day you just warm it slowly in the oven and serve. This one's a winner!

Ingredients

Vegetables

  • 4 large potatoes
  • 3 aubergines
  • 3 eggs, whisked with 60 ml milk
  • 500 ml dry breadcrumbs
  • olive oil

Meat

  • 1.2 kg beef or lamb mince
  • 2 onions, finely chopped
  • 4 garlic cloves, finely chopped
  • 150 ml red wine
  • 10 ml ground cinnamon
  • 5 ml allspice
  • 100 ml chopped parsley
  • 90 ml tomato paste
  • 1 tin tomato puree
  • 5 ml sugar
  • salt and pepper to taste

Bechamel sauce

  • 125 ml butter
  • 125 ml flour
  • 1.2 litre lukewarm milk
  • 4 egg yolks, lightly whisked (make meringue from the whites)
  • 2 ml nutmeg
  • salt and pepper
  • 100 – 150 g grated cheese (keep for last)

Method

Vegetables

Peel the potatoes and cook until just tender.

Slice into 1 cm rounds, arrange in a greased deep ovenproof dish, cover with cling film and set aside.

Slice the aubergine into 1 cm rounds and salt lightly.

Dip the aubergine in egg and then in breadcrumbs.

Place on a baking tray lined with foil.

Bake for about 30 minutes at 180 ºC. Turn regularly until done.

Remove from the oven and set aside.

Meat

Fry the onions and garlic in olive oil until translucent.

Brown the mince, breaking it up nicely with a fork.

Mix in the rest of the ingredients.

Simmer over low heat without a lid for about 1 hour or until nice and thick.

Bechamel sauce

Melt the butter and stir in the flour.

Stir in the milk slowly while whisking regularly with a balloon whisk.

Let it bubble until it thickens.

Remove from the heat and stir in the egg yolks and nutmeg.

Season to taste with salt and pepper.

Return to the heat and cook until nice and thick.

Build your Moussaka

Spoon the meat mixture on top of the potato layer.

Now arrange all the aubergine on top.

Pour the bechamel sauce over carefully.

Sprinkle with cheese.

Bake for 45 minutes at 180 ºC.

Let it stand for at least 30 minutes before slicing and serving.

Easy!
